goal: Planner persists a plan artifact and hands off to controlflow-verify via plan_path — controlflow-verify runs (or is skipped for TRIVIAL) based on tier

inputs.label: medium-plan-handoff
inputs.description: Planner receives a medium-complexity initial-create request, persists a plan artifact, and hands off to controlflow-verify. controlflow-verify runs its review phases.

input.query: Refactor the authentication module to use JWT tokens across 8 files
input.context: Medium complexity: cross-domain refactor touching auth, API, and config files. Planner should produce a plan and hand off to controlflow-verify.

inputs.label: trivial-plan-handoff
inputs.description: Planner receives a trivial initial-create request, persists a minimal plan, and hands off. controlflow-verify is skipped (TRIVIAL tier per runtime policy).

input.query: Fix the typo in README.md line 42
input.context: Trivial: single file, single concern. Planner produces a plan; controlflow-verify is skipped per the tier table.

assertions: Planner always persists an artifact and emits plan_path for controlflow-verify
Initial create uses revision_mode: initial_create when no active_plan_path exists
Planner handoff message includes complexity_tier so the verify-phase depth can be routed correctly
Planner handoff remains artifact-first and chat stays a concise summary instead of inlining the plan body
controlflow-verify does not treat plan_path handoff as implicit approval — it evaluates the review trigger conditions
TRIVIAL plans skip controlflow-verify per the tier table; MEDIUM plans run the full verify-phase set
Semantic-risk trigger heuristics remain anchored to plans/project-context.md even when Planner emits only the saved artifact and handoff summary
